CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-14-2008 CONTINUED TO APR-25-2008 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 212. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT W.W. WHARF GL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY DEMONSTRATING THAT THIS ACTION ARISING FROM PETITIONING ACTIVITY, CONDUCT IN THE COURSE OF PRIOR LITIGATION. TO THE EXTENT, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM SOUNDS IN TORT, C.C. 47 BARS THE CLAIM. TO THE EXTENT IT SOUNDS IN CONTRACT, IT MAY WELL BE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND IT IS BARRED BY COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF'S BRIEF ON APPEAL OF THE PRIOR CASE MAKES CLEAR THAT THE VERY ISSUE PRESENTED BY THIS COMPLAINT WAS LITIGATED IN THE PRIOR CASE AND A FINAL JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED IN THE PRIOR CASE REJECTING PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTIONS. ONE ORDER SIGNED ON THIS MOTION AND DEFENDANT JEFFREY LOWENTHAL'S MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JEFFREY LOWENTHAL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. TO THE EXTENT, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM SOUNDS IN TORT, C.C. 47 BARS THE CLAIM. TO THE EXTENT IT SOUNDS IN CONTRACT, IT MAY WELL BE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND IT IS BARRED BY COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF'S BRIEF ON APPEAL OF THE PRIOR CASE MAKES CLEAR THAT THE VERY ISSUE PRESENTED BY THIS COMPLAINT WAS LITIGATED IN THE PRIOR CASE AND A FINAL JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED IN THE PRIOR CASE REJECTING PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTIONS. ONE ORDER SIGNED ON THIS MOTION AND FOR DEFENDANT W.W. WHARF GL, INC.'S MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WW WHARF GL, INC'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS GRANTED AND THE MOTION FOR COSTS IS DENIED. W. W. WHARF IS AWARDED $23,300.00; LOWENTHAL IS AWARDED $17,000.00. THE COURT FINDS THE HOURLY RATES TO BE REASONABLE AND THAT WORK ON THE DEMURRER AND ANTI-SLAPP MOTION WERE INTERTWINED AND SUPPORTIVE OF THE ANTI-SLAPP ISSUES. THESE DEFENDANTS HAD A COMMON INTEREST AND MADE IDENTICAL ARGUMENTS. THE TOTAL HOURS SOUGHT, 135.5, IS EXCESSIVE. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION AND REDUCES THE HOURS BY 25%. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Pursuant To Cccp 416.5(C)
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JEFFREY LOWENTHAL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CCP 416.5(C)

No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion for attorneys fees is granted and the motion for costs is denied. W. W. Wharf is awarded $23,300.00; Lowenthal is awarded $17,000.00. The Court finds the hourly rates to be reasonable and that work on the demurrer and anti-SLAPP motion were intertwined and supportive of the anti-SLAPP issues. These defendants had a common interest and made identical arguments. The total hours sought, 135.5, is excessive. The Court exercises its discretion and reduces the hours by 25%. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT W.W. WHARF GL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL L. BOLI, ESQUIRE (510.535.9001) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. EDWARD EGAN SMITH, ESQUIRE (STEYER LOWENTHAL, ET AL., 415.421.3400) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY DEMONSTRATING THAT THIS ACTION ARISING FROM PETITIONING ACTIVITY, CONDUCT IN THE COURSE OF PRIOR LITIGATION. TO THE EXTENT, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM SOUNDS IN TORT, C.C. 47 BARS THE CLAIM. TO THE EXTENT IT SOUNDS IN CONTRACT, IT MAY WELL BE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND IT IS BARRED BY COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF'S BRIEF ON APPEAL OF THE PRIOR CASE MAKES CLEAR THAT THE VERY ISSUE PRESENTED BY THIS COMPLAINT WAS LITIGATED IN THE PRIOR CASE AND A FINAL JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED IN THE PRIOR CASE REJECTING PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTIONS. ONE ORDER SIGNED ON THIS MOTION AND DEFENDANT JEFFREY LOWENTHAL'S MOTION. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
